subject: science
question: What do plants need to make their food?
explanation: Plants make their own food through photosynthesis — using sunlight, water from the soil, and carbon dioxide from the air. They're amazing little food factories!

subject: science
question: What gas do plants give off that we breathe?
explanation: During photosynthesis, plants take in carbon dioxide and give out oxygen — the gas we need to breathe. Trees and plants are our oxygen factories!

subject: science
question: What is an invertebrate?
explanation: Invertebrates don't have a backbone (spine). Worms, snails, spiders, and insects are all invertebrates. About 97% of all animals are invertebrates!

subject: science
question: Where do tadpoles come from?
explanation: Tadpoles hatch from frog eggs (frogspawn). They gradually grow legs and lose their tails to become frogs — a process called metamorphosis!

subject: science
question: Which of these animals is a mammal? 🐾
explanation: Mammals have fur/hair, are warm-blooded, and feed their young with milk. Dolphins breathe air and give birth to live young — so they're mammals even though they live in the sea!

subject: science
question: What is the process by which a caterpillar becomes a butterfly?
explanation: Metamorphosis means a big change in body form. A caterpillar creates a chrysalis and transforms into a butterfly — one of nature's most magical processes!

subject: science
question: What do bees collect from flowers to make honey?
explanation: Bees collect nectar (a sweet liquid) from flowers and bring it back to the hive, where they turn it into honey. They also collect pollen which helps fertilise flowers!

subject: science
question: What is a food chain?
options: The order in which animals eat each other
A list of what animals eat
answer: The order in which animals eat each other
explanation: A food chain shows who eats what. For example: grass → rabbit → fox. Energy passes from one living thing to the next!

subject: science
question: What is hibernation?
explanation: Some animals like hedgehogs and bats hibernate in winter. Their body temperature drops and they sleep deeply to save energy when food is scarce. Irish hedgehogs are great hibernators!

subject: science
question: Which part of the plant absorbs water from the soil? 🌱
explanation: Roots anchor the plant in the soil and absorb water and minerals. The water then travels up through the stem to the leaves!

subject: science
question: Which of these is a natural material? 🌳
explanation: Wood comes directly from trees — it's a natural material. Plastic, glass, and metal foil are all processed or manufactured materials!

subject: science
question: What happens to water when it is heated to 100°C?
options: It boils and turns to steam
answer: It boils and turns to steam
explanation: Water boils at 100°C and turns into water vapour (steam). This is called evaporation. Steam is still water — just in a gas form!

subject: science
question: Which material is a good conductor of electricity?
explanation: Metals like copper are good conductors — electricity flows through them easily. That's why electrical wires are made of copper!

subject: science
question: What is the state of water at -10°C?
explanation: Water freezes at 0°C. At -10°C, water is solid ice! The three states of matter are solid, liquid, and gas.

subject: science
question: What do we call a material that does NOT let electricity pass through it?
explanation: Insulators block electricity — rubber, plastic, and wood are good insulators. That's why the handles of electrical tools are covered in rubber!

subject: science
question: Where does most of Earth's energy come from? ⚡
explanation: The Sun is our primary energy source. It powers plants through photosynthesis, creates wind, and warms our planet!

subject: science
question: What type of energy does a stretched elastic band have?
explanation: When you stretch an elastic band, you store energy in it. When you let go, that stored energy (potential energy) turns into movement (kinetic energy)!

subject: science
question: Which of these is a renewable energy source? 🌿
explanation: Wind is renewable — it never runs out! Coal, oil, and gas are fossil fuels that took millions of years to form and will run out one day. Ireland has lots of great wind energy!

subject: science
question: What does a solar panel do?
explanation: Solar panels contain special cells that convert light energy from the sun directly into electrical energy. More and more Irish homes are using them!

subject: science
question: What is sound?
options: A form of vibration that travels through air
answer: A form of vibration that travels through air
explanation: Sound is made by vibrations. When something vibrates (like a guitar string), it causes the air around it to vibrate too, creating sound waves that travel to our ears!

subject: science
question: What is the name of our planet?
explanation: We live on Earth — the third planet from the Sun! Earth is special because it has liquid water and supports life.

subject: science
question: How long does it take Earth to travel once around the Sun?
explanation: Earth takes approximately 365 days (one year) to orbit the Sun. That's why we have a year with 365 days!

subject: science
question: What causes the phases of the Moon (crescent, full moon, etc.)?
options: The moon turning on and off
The way the Sun lights different parts of the Moon as it orbits Earth
answer: The way the Sun lights different parts of the Moon as it orbits Earth
explanation: As the Moon orbits Earth, we see different amounts of its lit-up side — giving us the phases from new moon to full moon and back!

subject: science
question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
explanation: Mars looks red from space because its surface is covered in iron oxide (rust)! Scientists are studying Mars to see if humans could ever live there.

subject: science
question: Which group of animals has feathers and lays eggs?
explanation: Birds are the only animals with feathers, and they lay eggs in nests.

subject: science
question: A robin in an Irish garden is what kind of animal?
explanation: A robin has feathers, a beak and wings, so it is a bird.

subject: science
question: What do we call animals that have fur or hair and feed their babies milk?
explanation: Mammals have fur or hair and the mothers make milk to feed their young.

subject: science
question: Emilia sees a fish in a stream. How does a fish breathe under water?
explanation: Fish use special body parts called gills to take in oxygen from the water.

subject: science
question: Which feature do all insects have?
explanation: All insects have six legs, and their bodies are split into three parts.

subject: science
question: A frog is an amphibian. What does that mean?
options: It lives only in the sea
It can live both in water and on land
answer: It can live both in water and on land
explanation: Amphibians like frogs start life in water and can later live on land too.

subject: science
question: Which of these animals is a reptile?
explanation: A lizard has dry, scaly skin and is cold-blooded, which makes it a reptile.

subject: science
question: What kind of skin do most reptiles have?
explanation: Reptiles such as snakes and lizards have dry skin covered in scales.

subject: science
question: A bat flies in the night sky. What group does a bat belong to?
explanation: Even though a bat flies, it has fur and feeds its babies milk, so it is a mammal.

subject: science
question: Which of these is NOT a feature that birds share?
explanation: Birds have two legs, not six. Having six legs is a feature of insects.

subject: science
question: Emilia finds an animal with moist skin that lays its eggs in pond water. What group is it most likely in?
explanation: Amphibians like frogs and newts have moist skin and lay jelly-like eggs in water.

subject: science
question: How is a whale different from a fish, even though both live in the sea?
options: A whale breathes air with lungs
A whale lays eggs in water
answer: A whale breathes air with lungs
explanation: A whale is a mammal, so it must come to the surface to breathe air, unlike a fish.

subject: science
question: A snail and a worm are not insects. Why not?
options: They do not have six legs
answer: They do not have six legs
explanation: Insects must have six legs, but snails and worms have no legs at all.

subject: science
question: Which group does a ladybird belong to?
explanation: A ladybird has six legs and a body in three parts, so it is an insect.

subject: science
question: Reptiles and amphibians are both cold-blooded. What does cold-blooded mean?
options: Their body temperature changes with their surroundings
They only live in cold places
answer: Their body temperature changes with their surroundings
explanation: Cold-blooded animals warm up or cool down depending on the temperature around them.

subject: science
question: Which two features together would tell you an animal is a mammal?
options: Fur and feeding milk to young
answer: Fur and feeding milk to young
explanation: Only mammals have both fur or hair and the ability to feed their babies milk.

subject: science
question: A penguin cannot fly, yet scientists still call it a bird. Why?
options: It has feathers, wings and lays eggs
answer: It has feathers, wings and lays eggs
explanation: Birds are grouped by features like feathers and laying eggs, not by whether they can fly.

subject: science
question: Emilia sorts animals into groups. Which animal belongs in the same group as a salmon?
explanation: A trout, like a salmon, is a fish with gills and scales that lives in water.

subject: science
question: Which statement about classifying animals is correct?
options: All animals that live in water are fish
Whales are fish because they swim
Bats are birds because they fly
Animals are grouped by their body features, not just where they live
answer: Animals are grouped by their body features, not just where they live
explanation: Scientists group animals by features like fur, feathers or gills, not only by their home.

subject: science
question: A newly discovered animal has moist skin, lays eggs in water, and its young breathe with gills before growing lungs. Which group does it belong to?
explanation: Starting in water with gills then growing lungs to live on land is the life pattern of amphibians.

subject: science
question: Why might a turtle (a reptile) and a frog (an amphibian) be sorted into different groups even though both can swim?
options: The turtle has dry scaly skin and the frog has moist skin
The turtle lives only in the sea
answer: The turtle has dry scaly skin and the frog has moist skin
explanation: Reptiles have dry scaly skin while amphibians have moist skin, which helps tell the groups apart.

subject: science
question: What is the very first stage of a butterfly's life?
explanation: A butterfly's life begins as a tiny egg, often laid on a leaf.

subject: science
question: What hatches out of a butterfly's egg?
explanation: A small caterpillar hatches from the egg and starts eating leaves to grow.

subject: science
question: What is the very first stage of a frog's life?
explanation: A frog begins life as an egg in a jelly-like clump called frogspawn.

subject: science
question: What do we call a baby frog that has just hatched and lives in the water?
explanation: A tadpole is the young frog that swims in water and breathes with gills.

subject: science
question: What is the correct order of a butterfly's life cycle?
explanation: A butterfly goes from egg, to caterpillar, into a chrysalis, then out as a butterfly.

subject: science
question: What happens to a caterpillar inside a chrysalis?
options: It grows more legs and stays a caterpillar
explanation: Inside the chrysalis the caterpillar's body slowly changes into a butterfly.

subject: science
question: In a frog's life cycle, what comes just before the adult frog?
explanation: A froglet is a young frog with legs and a small tail that becomes a full-grown frog.

subject: science
question: Emilia spots jelly-like eggs in an Irish pond in spring. What are these called?
explanation: Frogspawn is the clump of jelly-covered frog eggs you often see in ponds in spring.

subject: science
question: As a tadpole grows into a froglet, which change happens?
options: It grows legs and loses its tail
answer: It grows legs and loses its tail
explanation: A tadpole slowly grows legs and its tail shrinks as it becomes a froglet.

subject: science
question: Why does a caterpillar eat so many leaves?
options: To grow big enough to form a chrysalis
answer: To grow big enough to form a chrysalis
explanation: A caterpillar eats lots of leaves so it grows big enough to change into a butterfly.

subject: science
question: How does a tadpole breathe when it first hatches?
explanation: A young tadpole uses gills to breathe under water, just like a fish.

subject: science
question: The big change in shape from caterpillar to butterfly is called what?
explanation: Metamorphosis is the word for the big body change a caterpillar makes to become a butterfly.

subject: science
question: Which stage of a frog's life happens entirely in the water?
explanation: The tadpole stage happens in water, where it swims and breathes with gills.

subject: science
question: Emilia watches a frog change as it grows. Which change shows it is ready to live on land?
explanation: Growing lungs to breathe air and legs to move means the frog can now live on land.

subject: science
question: Both a butterfly and a frog change shape as they grow. What is one big difference between their life cycles?
options: The butterfly has a chrysalis stage but the frog does not
The frog lays eggs but the butterfly does not
The butterfly stays in water its whole life
answer: The butterfly has a chrysalis stage but the frog does not
explanation: A butterfly forms a chrysalis to change, while a frog changes from tadpole to froglet without one.

subject: science
question: If Emilia found a chrysalis attached to a twig, what would she expect to come out of it next?
explanation: The chrysalis is the stage just before the adult, so a butterfly will emerge from it.

subject: science
question: Why is it important that frogs lay so many eggs at once?
options: Many eggs are eaten or do not survive, so laying lots helps some grow up
answer: Many eggs are eaten or do not survive, so laying lots helps some grow up
explanation: Lots of frogspawn is eaten by other animals, so laying many eggs means at least some become frogs.

subject: science
question: A pond dries up early in summer. Why could this be a serious problem for frogs?
options: Tadpoles need water to survive and grow
Frogspawn floats away on the wind
answer: Tadpoles need water to survive and grow
explanation: Tadpoles breathe and live in water, so a dried-up pond means they cannot grow into frogs.

subject: science
question: Emilia puts these butterfly stages in order by size: tiny egg, fat caterpillar, still chrysalis, winged butterfly. At which stage does the animal feed and grow the most?
options: It does not grow at any stage
explanation: The caterpillar eats constantly and grows the most, building up to change in the chrysalis.

subject: science
question: What do we call the natural home of an animal?
explanation: A habitat is the natural place where an animal lives and finds its food.

subject: science
question: Which habitat would you find an Irish woodland animal like a red squirrel living in?
explanation: A red squirrel lives in woodland, where it can climb trees and find nuts to eat.

subject: science
question: Which animal lives in an ocean habitat?
explanation: A dolphin lives in the ocean, swimming through salt water to find fish.

subject: science
question: A polar bear lives where it is very cold and snowy. What is this habitat called?
explanation: The Arctic is the cold, icy habitat at the top of the world where polar bears live.

subject: science
question: What does the word 'adaptation' mean?
options: A special feature that helps an animal live in its habitat
answer: A special feature that helps an animal live in its habitat
explanation: An adaptation is a body part or behaviour that helps an animal survive where it lives.

subject: science
question: How does a polar bear's thick fur help it survive?
options: It keeps the bear warm in the cold
It helps the bear swim faster
It keeps the bear cool in the heat
answer: It keeps the bear warm in the cold
explanation: Thick fur is an adaptation that traps heat and keeps the polar bear warm in the Arctic.

subject: science
question: A fish has fins. How do fins help a fish in its watery habitat?
options: They help it walk on land
They help it swim and steer
answer: They help it swim and steer
explanation: Fins are an adaptation that let a fish push through the water and change direction.

subject: science
question: Which animal would you most likely find in an Irish freshwater habitat like a river?
explanation: A trout is a fish that lives in fresh water such as Irish rivers and lakes.

subject: science
question: A camel lives in the desert. How does storing fat in its hump help it?
options: It gives the camel energy when food is scarce
It keeps sand out of its eyes
It helps it fly over the sand
answer: It gives the camel energy when food is scarce
explanation: The hump stores fat the camel can use for energy when there is little food in the desert.

subject: science
question: Why does an Arctic hare have a white coat in winter?
options: To stay warm in the sun
To camouflage and hide in the snow
answer: To camouflage and hide in the snow
explanation: A white coat blends in with the snow, helping the hare hide from animals that hunt it.

subject: science
question: Webbed feet help a duck do what in its watery habitat?
options: Push through the water like paddles
answer: Push through the water like paddles
explanation: Webbed feet act like paddles, helping the duck swim well in ponds and rivers.

subject: science
question: Emilia notices a frog has long back legs. How does this adaptation help it?
explanation: Strong, long back legs help a frog leap on land and push through water when it swims.

subject: science
question: Why do many woodland owls hunt at night?
options: They have adaptations like sharp hearing and good night vision
They are afraid of other birds
answer: They have adaptations like sharp hearing and good night vision
explanation: Owls are adapted to hunt at night with excellent hearing and eyes that see well in the dark.

subject: science
question: A desert fox has very large ears. Besides hearing, how do big ears help it in the hot desert?
options: They let body heat escape to keep it cool
answer: They let body heat escape to keep it cool
explanation: Large ears release extra body heat, helping the fox stay cool in the hot desert.

subject: science
question: Why would a fish struggle to survive if it were moved from the ocean onto dry land?
options: It is too cold on land
Its gills cannot take oxygen from the air
It would get too much sunlight
answer: Its gills cannot take oxygen from the air
explanation: Fish gills only work in water, so on land a fish cannot get the oxygen it needs to breathe.

subject: science
question: Emilia sees a hedgehog roll into a spiny ball. How does this adaptation help it?
options: Its spines protect it from animals that might attack
It keeps the hedgehog warm in summer
answer: Its spines protect it from animals that might attack
explanation: Rolling into a spiky ball protects the hedgehog's soft body from predators.

subject: science
question: A polar bear and a camel both have adaptations for their habitats. Which pair is matched correctly?
options: Polar bear: thick fur for cold; Camel: stores fat for the desert
Polar bear: large ears for heat; Camel: thick fur for cold
Polar bear: gills for water; Camel: webbed feet
Polar bear: humps for fat; Camel: white fur for snow
answer: Polar bear: thick fur for cold; Camel: stores fat for the desert
explanation: The polar bear's thick fur suits the cold Arctic and the camel's fat store suits the dry desert.

subject: science
question: A plant-eating animal moves to a new habitat where the only food is high in tall trees. Which adaptation would help it survive best?
options: A longer neck or the ability to climb to reach the leaves
answer: A longer neck or the ability to climb to reach the leaves
explanation: To reach food high up, an adaptation like a long neck or climbing skill would help it survive.

subject: science
question: Why might an animal perfectly adapted to the Arctic struggle if its habitat slowly became much warmer?
options: Its thick fur and fat would make it too hot, and its white colour would no longer hide it
It would have too much food
Warm weather has no effect on animals
answer: Its thick fur and fat would make it too hot, and its white colour would no longer hide it
explanation: Adaptations for cold can become a problem in warmth, leaving the animal overheated and easy to spot.

subject: science
question: In a food chain, what do we call a plant that makes its own food?
explanation: Plants are called producers because they make their own food using sunlight.

subject: science
question: What do we call an animal that eats other living things in a food chain?
explanation: A consumer is a living thing that eats plants or other animals to get energy.

subject: science
question: What is an animal called when it hunts and eats other animals?
explanation: A predator is an animal that hunts and eats other animals for food.

subject: science
question: In the food chain grass → rabbit → fox, what does the rabbit eat?
explanation: The arrows show the rabbit eats the grass, and then the fox eats the rabbit.

subject: science
question: What gives a plant the energy to make its own food?
explanation: Plants use the energy from sunlight to make their own food, which is why they are producers.

subject: science
question: In the food chain leaf → caterpillar → bird, which one is the producer?
explanation: The leaf comes from a plant, which makes its own food, so it is the producer.

subject: science
question: In an Irish field, grass → rabbit → fox is a food chain. Which animal is the predator?
explanation: The fox hunts and eats the rabbit, so the fox is the predator in this chain.

subject: science
question: Every food chain begins with a producer. Why is this so important?
options: Producers make the food energy that everything else depends on
answer: Producers make the food energy that everything else depends on
explanation: Plants capture the sun's energy to make food, and that energy passes to every animal in the chain.

subject: science
question: In the chain pond plant → tadpole → heron, where does the heron's energy first come from?
options: The sun, captured by the pond plant
answer: The sun, captured by the pond plant
explanation: The pond plant uses sunlight to make food, and that energy passes up the chain to the heron.

subject: science
question: An animal that is hunted and eaten by a predator is called what?
explanation: Prey is the name for an animal that is hunted and eaten by a predator.

subject: science
question: In the food chain seeds → mouse → owl, which animal is both a consumer and prey?
explanation: The mouse eats seeds so it is a consumer, but the owl eats the mouse, so it is also prey.

subject: science
question: In the food chain grass → rabbit → fox, what would most likely happen if all the grass disappeared?
options: The rabbits would have nothing to eat, and the foxes would lose their food too
The foxes would eat more grass
The grass would grow back instantly
answer: The rabbits would have nothing to eat, and the foxes would lose their food too
explanation: Without grass the rabbits have no food, and fewer rabbits means less food for the foxes.

subject: science
question: Emilia draws a food chain. Which order shows energy passing correctly?
explanation: Energy flows from the producer that makes food, to consumers, and on to the predators.

subject: science
question: In a woodland, foxes eat rabbits. If lots of new foxes arrived, what would likely happen to the number of rabbits at first?
options: The number of rabbits would go down
The number of rabbits would stay exactly the same
The rabbits would turn into foxes
answer: The number of rabbits would go down
explanation: More foxes would hunt and eat more rabbits, so the rabbit numbers would fall.

subject: science
question: In the chain grass → rabbit → fox, the number of foxes suddenly drops. What is the most likely first change?
options: The number of rabbits rises because fewer are eaten
The foxes come back the next day
answer: The number of rabbits rises because fewer are eaten
explanation: With fewer foxes hunting them, more rabbits survive, so their numbers rise for a while.

subject: science
question: Why can a food chain never start with a fox or other meat-eating animal?
options: A fox cannot make its own food, so the chain must begin with a producer like a plant
answer: A fox cannot make its own food, so the chain must begin with a producer like a plant
explanation: Only producers like plants can make food from sunlight, so every food chain must start with one.

subject: science
question: Which of these animals is a fish?
explanation: A herring has gills, fins and scales and lives in water, so it is a fish.

subject: science
question: A seal in Irish waters has fur and feeds its pups milk. What group is it in?
explanation: A seal has fur and feeds its young milk, which makes it a mammal even though it swims.

subject: science
question: How many legs does a spider have?
explanation: A spider has eight legs, which is why it is not an insect even though it is small.

subject: science
question: Why is a spider NOT classified as an insect?
options: It has eight legs instead of six
answer: It has eight legs instead of six
explanation: Insects have six legs, but a spider has eight, so it belongs to a different group.

subject: science
question: A bee visits flowers in an Irish garden. What group does a bee belong to?
explanation: A bee has six legs and a three-part body, so it is an insect.

subject: science
question: How does a bee help flowers when it moves from one to another?
options: It carries pollen and helps the flowers make seeds
answer: It carries pollen and helps the flowers make seeds
explanation: As a bee feeds, it carries pollen between flowers, helping them make seeds and fruit.

subject: science
question: Which of these is an amphibian found in Ireland?
explanation: The common frog is an Irish amphibian that lives both in water and on land.

subject: science
question: A newt and a frog are both amphibians. What do they have in common?
options: Both have moist skin and lay eggs in water
answer: Both have moist skin and lay eggs in water
explanation: Amphibians like newts and frogs share moist skin and lay their eggs in water.

subject: science
question: Emilia sees a hen at a farm. Why is a hen a bird?
options: It has feathers, wings and lays eggs
answer: It has feathers, wings and lays eggs
explanation: A hen has feathers, wings and lays eggs, which are all features of birds.

subject: science
question: Which list contains only mammals?
explanation: A dog, cow and bat all have fur and feed their young milk, so all three are mammals.

subject: science
question: Which list contains only animals that lay eggs?
explanation: A hen, a frog and a snake all lay eggs, while the others are mammals that give birth to live young.

subject: science
question: Which habitat is salty and very deep?
explanation: The ocean is a deep habitat full of salt water, home to fish, whales and many other animals.

subject: science
question: A grassland habitat is mostly covered in what?
explanation: A grassland is a wide open habitat covered in grasses with very few trees.

subject: science
question: Emilia finds a small Irish animal with a slimy moist body and no legs in damp soil. It is most likely a worm. Why is it NOT an insect?
options: It has no six legs and no three body parts
It comes out when it rains
answer: It has no six legs and no three body parts
explanation: Insects need six legs and a body in three parts, but a worm has neither.

subject: science
question: Emilia is given clues about a mystery Irish animal: it has fur, lives near rivers, has webbed feet, and feeds its babies milk. What is the best classification?
options: A mammal adapted for swimming, like an otter
answer: A mammal adapted for swimming, like an otter
explanation: Fur and feeding milk make it a mammal, and webbed feet are an adaptation for swimming, like an otter.

subject: science
question: Where does food begin its journey through the digestive system?
explanation: Digestion starts in the mouth, where teeth chew the food into smaller pieces.

subject: science
question: What do your teeth do to food in your mouth?
explanation: Teeth break food into small pieces so it is easier to swallow and digest.

subject: science
question: Which body part is like a stretchy bag that mixes up your food?
explanation: The stomach is a stretchy bag that churns and mixes food to break it down.

subject: science
question: What is the name of the tube that carries food from your mouth down to your stomach?
explanation: The oesophagus is the tube that pushes swallowed food down to the stomach.

subject: science
question: After the stomach, where does food go next?
explanation: From the stomach, food moves into the small intestine, where goodness is taken into the body.

subject: science
question: What is the main job of the small intestine?
options: To take the goodness from food into the body
answer: To take the goodness from food into the body
explanation: The small intestine soaks up the useful nutrients from food so the body can use them.

subject: science
question: What does the large intestine mainly do?
options: Takes water from leftover food and stores waste
answer: Takes water from leftover food and stores waste
explanation: The large intestine takes back water from the leftovers and stores the waste until it leaves the body.

subject: science
question: What is the correct order food travels through the digestive system?
options: Mouth → oesophagus → stomach → small intestine → large intestine
Mouth → stomach → oesophagus → large intestine → small intestine
Stomach → mouth → small intestine → large intestine
answer: Mouth → oesophagus → stomach → small intestine → large intestine
explanation: Food goes mouth, down the oesophagus, into the stomach, then the small and large intestines.

subject: science
question: Why is it important to chew your food well before swallowing?
options: Smaller pieces are easier for the stomach to break down
It makes the food taste sweeter
answer: Smaller pieces are easier for the stomach to break down
explanation: Chewing into small pieces gives the stomach an easier job breaking the food down.

subject: science
question: Emilia takes a bite of her apple. Which part of her body adds spit (saliva) to start breaking the food down?
explanation: The mouth makes saliva, which mixes with chewed food and starts to break it down.

subject: science
question: What happens to the parts of food the body cannot use?
options: They become waste and leave the body
They go back to the mouth
answer: They become waste and leave the body
explanation: The parts the body cannot use pass through the large intestine and leave as waste.

subject: science
question: How does food move down the oesophagus even when you are lying down?
options: The muscles squeeze and push the food along
You blow it down with air
answer: The muscles squeeze and push the food along
explanation: Muscles in the oesophagus squeeze in waves to push food to the stomach, even upside down.

subject: science
question: Which statement about the small and large intestines is correct?
options: The small intestine takes in nutrients; the large intestine takes back water
The small intestine pumps blood; the large intestine breathes
The large intestine chews food; the small intestine stores it
Both organs make the heart beat
answer: The small intestine takes in nutrients; the large intestine takes back water

question: In the chain plant → snail → thrush, which animal is eaten by the thrush?
explanation: The thrush eats the snail, which had eaten the plant.

subject: science
question: Why are producers like plants always at the start of a food chain?
options: They make food using sunlight, which feeds everything else
answer: They make food using sunlight, which feeds everything else
explanation: Plants make their own food from sunlight, providing energy that all the consumers depend on.

subject: science
question: Penguins have a thick layer of fat and waterproof feathers. What habitat are they suited to?
options: Cold, icy places near the sea
answer: Cold, icy places near the sea
explanation: Thick fat and waterproof feathers keep penguins warm and dry in cold, icy seaside places.

subject: science
question: Which of these best describes adaptation?
options: Features that help an animal survive in its habitat
The home where an animal lives
answer: Features that help an animal survive in its habitat
explanation: Adaptations are special features or behaviours that help an animal survive where it lives.

subject: science
question: Which of these animals has gills and lives its whole life in water?
explanation: A trout is a fish, so it has gills and lives in water all its life.

subject: science
question: Emilia studies a pond and lists all the living things and how they connect. What is she studying?
options: A food web of the pond habitat
answer: A food web of the pond habitat
explanation: By showing how living things feed on each other, Emilia is studying the pond's food web.

subject: science
question: Why is it important to protect habitats like Irish woodlands and wetlands?
options: Many living things depend on them to survive
They have no animals in them
answer: Many living things depend on them to survive
explanation: Habitats provide food, water and shelter for many plants and animals, so protecting them keeps those living things alive.

subject: science
question: What is the process called where plants make their own food using sunlight? Type your answer.
explanation: Photosynthesis is the process where plants use sunlight, water and carbon dioxide to make glucose (food) and release oxygen.

subject: science
question: How many bones does the adult human body have? Type the number.
explanation: The adult human body has 206 bones. Babies are born with about 270-300 bones, but many fuse together as we grow!

subject: science
question: Light travels at approximately how many thousand kilometres per second? Slide to guess!
explanation: Light travels at approximately 300,000 km per second — fast enough to circle the Earth seven times in one second!

subject: science
question: Your heart beats approximately how many times per minute when resting? Slide to guess!
explanation: A healthy resting heart beats about 60-100 times per minute — around 70 bpm on average. During exercise it can double!

subject: science
question: Select ALL the animals that are mammals. 🐾
explanation: Dolphins and bats are mammals — warm-blooded, breathe air, and nurse their young on milk. Salmon are fish; geckos are reptiles.

subject: science
question: Select ALL the things that are LIVING. 🌱
explanation: The oak tree and the bee are living things — they both carry out life processes (movement, growth, reproduction etc.). Rocks and water are non-living.

subject: science
question: Which of these is the largest animal that has ever lived on Earth?
explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal that has ever lived — up to 30 metres long and 150 tonnes. Its heart alone is the size of a small car!

subject: science
question: Which part of the plant absorbs water and minerals from the soil?
explanation: Roots anchor the plant in the soil and absorb water and minerals that travel up the stem to the rest of the plant.

subject: science
question: Which force pulls everything towards the centre of the Earth?
explanation: Gravity is the force that pulls all objects towards the Earth. It is why things fall when you drop them and why you stay on the ground!
